name: Dan Wellner-Green Chile Stew
recipeInstructions:
1. Flour the pork and brown it in the drippings in a skillet.
2. Remove the pork to a large stew pot, 3 to 5 quart size. Add onions to the drippings in the skillet and cook until the onion is translucent.
3. Add the onions to the pork. Cut the chiles into 1 inch slices; add to the meat and onions in the pot.
4. Add the remaining ingredients and cook over medium heat for about 1 hour, until the stew is slightly thickened.
5. If you prefer the all day method, follow the same directions except put the ingredients in a crock pot. Cook on low for 10-12 hours or high for 5 hours.
6. I like using fresh jalapenos. Depending on where the chiles are from you can have a rather fiery brew!!! Make sure you have plenty of flour tortillas handy, you will not want to miss one tasty drop.
